title: Pronephrium hewittii
taxonomy: Pronephrium hewittii is classified as a species of fern in the family Marattiaceae. It belongs to the genus Pronephrium, which consists of several species of epiphytic and terrestrial ferns. The family Marattiaceae comprises over 200 species of ferns, mostly found in tropical regions. Pronephrium hewittii is native to Southeast Asia, where it grows in shaded, humid environments. This species is known for its unique, palmate leaves that help it thrive in its native habitat.

intro: Pronephrium hewittii, also known as Hewitt's fern or Pronephrium, is a species of fern in the family Marattiaceae, characterized by its large, leathery fronds and distinctive leaf structure. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in the Philippines, to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its reported health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Pronephrium hewittii include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Pronephrium hewittii is commonly prepared as a tea or infusions, and is also used in traditional remedies such as decoctions and tinctures to harness its purported therapeutic benefits.
